# Automation Signals Worth Watching

## Media

![Newsletter reference](/demo/library-newsletter.svg)

## Summary

This newsletter candidate lists small automation signals that are easy to miss until they repeat across several sources.

## Key Points

- Watch for repeated workflow friction across meetings and bookmarks.
- Promote candidate notes only when they are likely to affect a project.
- Keep source metadata visible so weak captures can be repaired later.

## Assessment

- Recommendation: review
- Why: Discovery source with product and automation overlap.
- What changed:
- What is suspect:

## Suggested Connections

- [[quarterly-review/index|Quarterly Review]]

## Raw Content

<details>
<summary>Full source cache</summary>

Teams often miss automation opportunities because each signal looks small on its own. A customer complaint, a meeting note, and a saved link become more valuable when they are read together. The best systems keep low-intent discovery cheap while making strong signals easy to save permanently.

</details>
